stemHtml:
Refer to figure. 
A runway has the following approaches available: an ILS (minima 200 ft) and a GNSS LNAV/VNAV (minima 250 ft). A new crane is erected in the approach area, increasing the OCH by 50 ft, resulting in a new OCH of 216 ft. Based on this information, which statement is correct?

options.a: You can continue both approaches without revising the minima
options.b: You cannot conduct either approach with the published minima, so both must be revised
options.c: The minima for the ILS must be revised due to the crane, but the GNSS (LNAV/VNAV) minima do not need to be changed
options.d: The minima for the GNSS approach must be revised due to the crane, but the ILS is sufficiently accurate and does not require a change
